title: dactylorhiza × transiens subsp. corylensis
intro: Dactylorhiza × transiens subsp. corylensis, also known as the Cory's Spotted Orchid, is a hybrid orchid species that is native to Europe and is characterized by its distinctive spotted leaves and tall flowering stem. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The Cory's Spotted Orchid has been used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems. Additionally, the plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas and salves, to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin.
